name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram Brooklyn Center, Minnesota — MoneyGram is a global money transfer service offering domestic and international fund transfers, bill payments, a...
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer company that operates through a distributed network of retail agent locations rather than as a standalone storefront. The company enables customers to send and receive money domestically and internationally by partnering with grocery stores, convenience stores, supermarkets, and other retail establishments. In the Brooklyn Park, Minnesota area alone, MoneyGram operates through seven agent locations including major retailers like Walmart and Cub Foods, as well as independent grocers and food markets.

MoneyGram's core service offerings center on money transfers—both domestic and international—along with complementary financial services typical of check-cashing operations. Customers can send funds to recipients, receive incoming transfers, and complete transactions either online or in-person at agent locations. The service emphasizes convenience and accessibility by operating extended hours through partner retailers (many open until 8-11 PM) and allowing customers to manage transfers through multiple channels.

What distinguishes MoneyGram is its agent-based model rather than company-owned branches. This approach provides widespread geographic coverage—seven locations within a few miles in one Minnesota suburb—and integrates money transfer services into existing retail shopping trips. The company's website emphasizes ease of use and the ability to track transfers online, positioning itself as a modern alternative to traditional wire transfer methods.

However, customers should note that MoneyGram does not operate its own physical locations; all transactions occur through third-party retail agents, which may mean variable service quality and hours depending on the host retailer. Transfer fees and exchange rates for international transfers are not disclosed on the location-finder page, and customers must contact individual locations or visit the main website for pricing. The service is most practical for those already shopping at partner retailers or in areas with good agent density.
address: 6810 N Humboldt Ave, Brooklyn Center, MN 55430

company_info.headquarters: 6810 N Humboldt Ave, Brooklyn Center, MN 55430

pros: Extensive local network—7 locations within 2-3 miles in Brooklyn Park area for convenient access
Extended operating hours through retail partners, with some locations open until 11 PM
Online and offline transfer options allowing customers to manage transactions flexibly
Integration with major retailers (Walmart, Cub Foods) and independent grocers for one-stop convenience
Domestic and international money transfer capabilities through single platform
Multiple agent location types (supermarkets, food markets, convenience stores) increase accessibility
Ability to send and receive funds without needing a bank account
cons: Fees and exchange rates not transparent on location-finder website; customers must contact locations individually
Dependent on third-party retail agents means service quality varies by location and retailer policies
No company-owned locations limits control over customer experience and service consistency
International transfer costs and rates may be less competitive than bank wire transfers
Limited information provided on the website about transfer speed, limits, or promotional offers
best_for: Customers needing quick domestic money transfers without a bank account
Immigrants and international families sending remittances to relatives abroad
People who prefer in-person transactions and want to combine money transfer with grocery shopping
Those seeking extended-hours access to money transfer services in suburban areas
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need fast domestic money transfers or want to send remittances internationally, particularly those in areas with good retail agent density. The main caveat is that all transactions depend on third-party retailers, so service quality, hours, and fees vary by location and are not consistently disclosed online.
services: International money transfers and remittance services
